# Zoom License Review

**Review date:** 2024-08-20  
**Owner:** Finance / IT Operations  
**Classification:** Confidential

## Current Contract

- **Plan:** Zoom Workplace Business Plus
- **Licensed hosts:** 1,800
- **Annual cost:** $207,000 (~$115/host/yr)
- **Contract expiry:** 2025-01-31  ← **Renewal imminent**

## Product Lines

| Product | Included? | Notes |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| Zoom Meetings | ✅ | Core product |
| Zoom Phone | ✅ | 340 users on Zoom Phone |
| Zoom Webinars | Partial | 5-pack add-on; $12,000/yr |
| Zoom Rooms | Add-on | 8 conference rooms; $5,760/yr |

**Total Zoom spend:** $224,760/yr

## Utilization

- Average daily meeting hosts (Zoom data): 1,190 of 1,800 licensed (66%)
- Zoom Phone active users: 338 of 340
- Webinar capacity utilization: moderate (3 of 5 licenses regularly used)

The 600+ underused meeting licenses are partly explained by contractor/seasonal worker accounts. IT Ops plans to add Zoom to the automated deprovisioning workflow by end of year.

## Competitor Check

Google Meet is included in our Google Workspace license. A subset of teams already uses it for quick calls. Full migration would require:
- Hardware reconfiguration for 8 conference rooms (~$24,000 one-time)
- Change management effort (moderate)
- Zoom Phone migration to Google Voice — more complex, needs separate evaluation

Net: hybrid scenario (keep Zoom Phone, move meetings to Google Meet) could save ~$85,000/yr. Worth modeling formally before January renewal.

## Recommended Actions

- [ ] IT Ops: implement automated Zoom seat deprovisioning by 2024-11-01
- [ ] Finance: model hybrid Google Meet / Zoom Phone scenario
- [ ] Procurement: request Zoom's best renewal pricing by 2024-10-31
- [ ] IT: assess conference room hardware for Google Meet compatibility
